title: Would you try the new couch-style seating on United? Here’s what readers said.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines plans to introduce couch-style economy seating, called Relax Row, in 2027.
In an informal poll, 61% of respondents expressed interest in trying the new seating option.
Concerns include potential for noise, pricing, and in-flight safety with the Relax Row concept.

title: This first grade teacher is running the Boston Marathon to support her students
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Siobhan Salani, a Weymouth teacher, is running the Boston Marathon to support the Corey C. Griffin Foundation.
The Foundation provides valuable opportunities for students at Saint John Paul II Catholic Academy Lower Mills.
Salani is honored to run for the foundation that significantly impacts her students' lives in Boston.

title: A Maine beach is among the best in the U.S. in 2026, according to Conde Nast Traveler
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Sand Beach at Acadia National Park, Maine, named among America's best beaches for 2026 by Conde Nast Traveler.
Sand Beach is unique for its pale sandy shores, cold Atlantic waters, and picturesque granite cliffs on Mount Desert Island.
Conde Nast Traveler suggests staying at Under Canvas Acadia or The Asticou Hotel for visitors to Sand Beach.

title: 16-year-old arrested in Allston shooting
author: John Waller
bullets: A 16-year-old was arrested in Allston for a shooting on Brighton Avenue that injured another juvenile.
The suspect faces multiple charges, including assault with intent to murder and carrying a firearm without a license.
The shooting victim was hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries; the suspect turned himself in after a warrant was issued.

title: Alex Cora calls Terry Francona a ‘Hall of Fame manager’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Alex Cora praised Terry Francona as a 'Hall of Fame manager' during the Red Sox's season opener against the Cincinnati Reds.
Terry Francona managed the Red Sox from 2004-2011, winning two World Series titles; he's now with the Cincinnati Reds.
The Red Sox lost two out of three games against the Reds in their opening series, heading next to play the Houston Astros.

title: Victim tied up by armed intruder during Beverly home invasion, police say
author: John Waller
bullets: An armed intruder broke into a Beverly home, tied up the occupant, and stole a vehicle, police report.
The incident occurred on Paine Avenue; police were called at 8:50 a.m. after a neighbor dialed 911.
The victim, who suffered non-life-threatening injuries, received treatment at Beverly Hospital and was later released.

title: After ‘No Kings’ rally, local organizers are looking to sustain momentum. What’s next?
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 180,000 attended the 'No Kings' rally in Boston Common, with organizers urging continued weekly civic engagement.
Organizers promote the 'We Are Powerful Pledge' for weekly actions like boycotts and advocacy, connecting with 50 local groups.
Plans for May Day events include economic protests and support for unions; Mass 50501 pushes data privacy activism.

title: Skier dies after fall at Sugarbush
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A skier died after falling at Sugarbush Resort, Vermont, on Saturday as reported by authorities.
The skier was found unresponsive off a trail on Stein's Run and later pronounced dead by the Mad River Valley Ambulance.
Police stated the incident at Sugarbush is not considered suspicious; an autopsy will determine the exact cause of death.

title: As playoffs near, doubt the 2025-26 Bruins at your own risk
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins rallied to a 4-3 shootout victory against the Columbus Blue Jackets, boosting playoff odds to 92%.
Viktor Arvidsson scored the decisive shootout goal, marking his 21st goal of the season for the resilient Bruins.
Boston's recent performances have secured six points against top NHL teams, indicating their playoff readiness despite past challenges.

title: Dating back to the 1600s, the second-oldest home in Cambridge lists for $5.8M
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The Hooper-Lee-Nichols House, built circa 1685, is listed for sale at $5.8 million in Cambridge.
Owned by History Cambridge since 1957, the house has served as its headquarters for 70 years.
The property has a preservation restriction to maintain its historical integrity, featuring Georgian to Colonial Revival elements.
